text: A mobile unit in distress should transmit the appropriate alarm signal followed by the distress call and message on one or all international distress frequencies. Which of the following sets of frequencies is correct?
explanation: The frequency 500 kHz (radiotelegraphy), 2182 kHz (radiotelephony), and 156.8 MHz (VHF Channel 16) are the designated international distress and calling frequencies. While technology has shifted toward DSC, these remain the core frequencies for distress alerting.
